column7: Description
Award-winning pianist Yifei Xu captivates audiences with her virtuosity, lyricism, and seamless fusion of classical and avant-garde styles.

Boyd Meets Girl pairs Australian classical guitarist Rupert Boyd with American cellist Laura Metcalf. The duo has toured the world sharing their eclectic mix of music from Debussy and Bach to Radiohead and Beyoncé, and their two studio albums have received over 4 million streams on Spotify alone.

Led by Lev “Ljova” Zhurbin on the six-string fadolín, with Sabina Torosjan on violin and Valeriya Sholokhova on cello, Trio Fadolín crafts an innovative chamber music experience, drawing on rich musical traditions from Eastern Europe, Scandinavia, and Latin-inspired genres.

Victoria Paterson leads the hugely popular FAB5! She brings a rich diversity of musicians & styles with pop music on classical instruments, featuring the string quartet & cajon. Top songs and themes from BBC Planet Earth, The Crown, The Godfather, James Bond, Game of Thrones, Rocky, and more!
Harold O'Neal, hailed as one of this generation's greatest pianists and composers, delivers an unforgettable performance, blending the brilliance of Duke Ellington, Kenny Kirkland, and Maurice Ravel.

The Big Apple Quintet, featuring Broadway’s top musicians and led by oboist Keve Wilson and violinist Rachel Handman, delights audiences with a vibrant fusion of global sounds, blending Irish jigs, Venezuelan joropos, Brazilian samba, and Persian folk melodies.
Enhance your commute with an invigorating performance as Latin GRAMMY Award winner Pedro Giraudo and his trio bring the rich traditions and innovative sounds of Argentine tango to life.

Justin Rothberg's trio delivers a foot-tapping fusion of pop, hip-hop, Afro-Cuban, and funky jazz, featuring NYC's top musicians and fresh takes on classics by Michael Jackson, Stevie Wonder, Soundgarden, and more.

The Pablo Mayor Trio creates a dynamic tapestry of global Latin American rhythms, rooted in the innovative vision of Folklore Urbano NYC's founder and celebrated Columbian composer and pianist Pablo Mayor.
Harold O'Neal, hailed as one of this generation's greatest pianists and composers, delivers an unforgettable performance, blending the brilliance of Duke Ellington, Kenny Kirkland, and Maurice Ravel.

Pianist Yifei Xu and cellist Matthew Armbruster celebrate the Lunar New Year with a selection of evocative pieces that reflect the beauty, energy, and traditions of the season. The program blends Eastern and Western influences, weaving together classical compositions and folk melodies to honor the spirit of renewal and joy.

Trio Fadolín performs an eclectic repertoire from around the world and across the ages, featuring music from Eastern Europe, Scandinavia, Latin-inspired grooves and much more, all featuring the fadolín, a unique string instrument that encompasses the range of the violin, viola and cello.

Celebrate the romance and passion of tango this Valentine’s Day season with an invigorating performance by Latin GRAMMY Award winner Pedro Giraudo’s quartet. Infused with the rich traditions and vibrant energy of Argentine tango, this performance offers the perfect soundtrack for the season of love.

Bobby Harden, a dynamic soul and blues artist, is a Blues Hall of Fame Inductee and the lead singer of The Original Blues Brothers Band and Bobby Harden & Soul Purpose. Renowned for his soulful, funky performances and incomparable energy, he has captivated audiences worldwide, from iconic stages to community settings. With accompanist Ron Thompson.

Justin Rothberg's trio delivers a foot-tapping fusion of pop, hip-hop, Afro-Cuban, and funky jazz, featuring NYC's top musicians and fresh takes on classics by Michael Jackson, Stevie Wonder, Soundgarden, and more.

Victoria Paterson leads the hugely popular FAB5! She brings a rich diversity of musicians & styles with pop music on classical instruments, featuring the string quartet & cajon. Top songs and themes from BBC Planet Earth, The Crown, The Godfather, James Bond, Game of Thrones, Rocky, and more!

Cellist/vocalist Kely Pinheiro and violinist Fabienne Jean bring their shared love for music and deep friendship to the stage, blending pop, rock, Brazilian and jazz influences in a way that feels fresh and deeply personal. With nothing but their strings, voices and a whole lot of heart, they reimagine songs with raw energy, soul and spontaneity. More than just making music, Kely and Fabienne want to create in a space where women in music feel seen, heard, and empowered - one song at a time.

Born and raised in Nagoya, Japan, Martha Kato is a pianist, composer, and arranger whose blossoming career in New York has taken her to stages around the world. Based in New York since 2009, she has performed as both bandleader and collaborator at major venues including Blue Note, Jazz Standard, Blue Note Tokyo, Cotton Club Japan, The Jazz Gallery, Dizzy’s Club, Smoke Jazz & Supper Club, and Smalls Jazz Club. An Artist Partner with Sing for Hope, Martha brings her artistry to public settings and older adult day centers, sharing music that connects and uplifts. She performs across a wide range of styles and instrumentations, and has shared the stage and studio with collaborators such as Remy Le Boeuf, Miho Hazama, Takuya Kuroda, Alexis Cuadrado, and Pedro Giraudo.

Arbor Duo, featuring soprano Marisa Karchin and classical guitarist Dr. Alberta Khoury, explores the essence of love and its countless expressions through a tapestry of languages and music across time. Their repertoire spans cultures worldwide, from Japan and Iran to Europe and Ladino traditions, weaving together diverse musical voices. United by a deep and lasting friendship formed at Juilliard, they bring passion and artistry to every performance, blending expressive storytelling with the guitar’s rich tonal colors and extended techniques.

Christine Cornell and Justin Rothberg bring their unique artistry to rock, folk, and country classics. Christine, a dynamic vocalist and actor, has performed on renowned stages from Broadway to international concert halls, captivating audiences with her stunning soprano voice. Justin, an in-demand NYC-based guitarist, is the current Broadway chair holder for Sunset Boulevard and has played for numerous productions, tours, and recording projects. Together, they create a fresh, expressive take on timeless songs, blending vocal elegance with masterful guitar work.

FAB5 features a mostly female cast from Japan, France, Ireland and the Ukraine!
March 13th is all about getting in the spirit of St Patrick's Day featuring songs from The Cranberries and Enya alongside the best Irish, Scandinavian and American folk favorites.

Join pianist Camila Cortina (Cuba), saxophonist Berta Moreno (Spain), and percussionist Takafumi Nikaido (Japan) as they come together to celebrate the vibrant connections between jazz and the rich traditions of Afro-Cuban, South American, and Flamenco music.

As part of Sing for Hope's March celebration of Women in Music, this concert will also pay tribute to the often-overlooked contributions of women composers in Latin American and jazz history. Through a dynamic fusion of rhythm and melody, these world-class musicians will take the audience on a journey through the heart and soul of Latin music, reimagining timeless classics and uncovering hidden gems.

This month at Moynihan, Trio Fadolín -- featuring violinist Sabina Torosjan, cellist Valeriya Sholokhova, and Ljova performing on the fadolín -- will perform works by the Spanish-American composer Andrea Casarrubios, Dutch composer Emmy Wegener, Armenian-American composer lieder by Fanny Mendelssohn, as well as songs from the repertoire of Celia Cruz, Nina Simone, Adele and others.

Friends since the age of 10, Rachel Handman (violin) and Keve Wilson (oboe) lead the Big Apple Band, made up of Broadway’s star musicians, entertains audiences with music from around the world. This oboe, violin, guitar, bass and drums combo spins irish jigs with Venezuelan joropos and Brazilian samba with Persian folk.

With nothing but their strings, voices and a whole lot of heart, they reimagine songs with raw energy, soul and spontaneity. More than just making music, Kely and Fabienne want to create in a space where women in music feel seen, heard, and empowered - one song at a time.

Top songs and themes from BBC Planet Earth, The Crown, The Godfather, James Bond, Game of Thrones, Rocky, and more!

Flutist Anjali Shinde and oboist Matthew Maroon come together with the simple goal of sharing meaningful music with as many people as possible. Their classical training pairs with a casual and open-minded nature, allowing them to connect across genre and locale to present accessible slices of musical joy to your day. Look forward to hearing a variety of their favorite musical selections, including their own arrangements, spanning baroque and classical duo sonatas, opera arias, jazz standards, and more

Guitarists Eduardo Gutterres and Gabriele Leite bring their deep connection and love for Brazilian music to the stage, blending the rich traditions of Bossa Nova, Samba, and classical guitar with their own distinct voice. Since moving to New York together in 2021, they have been performing as a duo, sharing their passion through original compositions, arrangements, and iconic works for two guitars.

Their journey has taken them to stages across the U.S., Brazil, and Europe, including performances at the United Nations Security Council’s meeting and the Aspen Music Festival. With spontaneity, charisma, and a deep love for their craft, they create performances that are both vibrant and heartfelt.

column4: Neel Murgai and Tripp Dudley - Brooklyn Raga Massive
column7: Neel Murgai (sitar) and Tripp Dudley (tabla) will be performing afternoon ragas and other delights from South Asian classical traditions and beyond. Neel and Tripp have a dynamic connection built from years of playing together in various incarnations with the Brooklyn Raga Massive, a musician's collective dedicated to raga and raga inspired music, where Neel is Artistic Director. Their latest group is the psychedelic raga rock band Quadrature, who will be releasing their debut album in 2025, showcasing their genre fluid expertise.

Boyd Meets Girl pairs Australian classical guitarist Rupert Boyd with American cellist Laura Metcalf. The duo has toured the world sharing their eclectic mix of music from Debussy and Bach to Radiohead and Beyoncé, and their two studio albums have received over 4 million streams on Spotify alone.
Enhance your commute with an invigorating performance as Latin GRAMMY Award winner Pedro Giraudo and his quartet bring the rich traditions and innovative sounds of Argentine tango to life.

Accordionist Simone Baron and fadolínist Ljova, both accomplished composers and collaborators, perform a set mixing original compositions with side-trips into jazz, klezmer, and Brazilian music for their debut on the Moynihan Sing for Hope Stage.

Arbor Duo, featuring soprano Marisa Karchin and classical guitarist Dr. Alberta Khoury, explores the essence of love and its countless expressions through a tapestry of languages and music across time. Their repertoire spans cultures worldwide, from Japan and Iran to Europe and Ladino traditions, weaving together diverse musical voices. United by a deep and lasting friendship formed at Juilliard, they bring passion and artistry to every performance, blending expressive storytelling with the guitar’s rich tonal colors and extended techniques.

Yi-Ting Liu and Yinze (Frank) Wang are Guqin musicians and visual artists based in New York. The Guqin, a seven-stringed zither, is an ancient Chinese instrument traditionally associated with scholars and literati, known for its serene and contemplative tones. Co-presented with ALT Alliance.

Yi-Ting Liu is a Guqin musician and visual artist based in New York. She holds MA and MFA degrees in oil painting. A member of the New York Guqin Association and Melody of Dragon, she has performed at venues including the Interchurch Center and Queens Public Library. Since 2023, she has studied Guqin with master Liu Li. She also explores the integration of art, music, and technology, recently designing a stage video for Luo Shen from Clouds. 

Yinze (Frank) Wang has been nurtured by Chinese calligraphy studies with his father since childhood. He internalized the essence of Eastern aesthetics through ink and brush. During his New York residency, he apprenticed for six years under guqin master Wensong Bai, inheriting the refined literati tradition. His performances bridge eras, channeling the surging torrents of Flowing Water, the serene tranquility of Wild Geese Descending on Sandbars, and the resilient spirit of Three Variations on the Plum Blossom.

Drift Winds brings flutist Anjali Shinde, oboist Matthew Maroon, and bassoonist Marty Tung together with the simple goal of sharing meaningful music with as many people as possible. Their classical training pairs with a casual and open-minded nature, allowing Drift to connect across genre and locale to bring accessible slices of musical joy to your day.

Look forward to hearing a variety of our favorite musical selections, including our own arrangements, baroque and classical trio sonatas, opera arias, jazz standards, and more.

Friends since the age of 10, Rachel Handman (violin) and Keve Wilson (oboe) lead the Big Apple Band, made up of Broadway’s star musicians, entertains audiences with music from around the world. This oboe, violin, guitar, bass and drums combo spins irish jigs with Venezuelan joropos and Brazilian samba with Persian folk.

Don’t miss a live set by pianist, composer, and vocalist Martha Kato, joined by trumpeter Takuya Kuroda and bassist Pablo Menares. Originally from Nagoya, Japan and now based in New York, Kato has performed internationally as a bandleader and collaborator. The trio will perform a mix of her original works and jazz favorites.

Victoria Paterson leads Mostly Modern's hugely popular FAB5! She brings a rich diversity of musicians & styles with pop music on classical instruments, featuring the string quartet & cajon. Top songs and themes from BBC Planet Earth, The Crown, The Godfather, James Bond, Game of Thrones, Rocky, and more!

Pedro Fortunato is a Dominican-American artist, producer, and musician who defies industry labels. He was born and raised in New York City and brings a fresh perspective to tropical contemporary music by embracing his roots in the diaspora, and incorporating both cultures into his art. His first single, "Palomita", earned him a nomination for the Premios Indie Awards in 2022. Co-presented with Musical Theater Factory.

column4:
Nicola Vazquez and
Simon Gomez Villegas
column7: Co-presented with Musical Theatre Factory, this rush-hour double bill brings two dynamic solo sets to the heart of Moynihan Train Hall. Bogotá-born multi-instrumentalist Simón Gómez Villegas shares an intimate blend of folk, theatre, and Americana—his genre-blurring storytelling shaped by global stages and a theatrical soul. Billboard-featured pop/rock artist Nicola Vazquez delivers powerhouse vocals and guitar-driven soul, with a magnetic presence and chart-topping songwriting that’s lit up venues from Madison Square Garden to MTV. Two distinct artists, one vibrant afternoon soundtrack.

Trio Fadolín performs an eclectic repertoire from around the world and across the ages, featuring music from Eastern Europe, Scandinavia, Latin-inspired grooves and much more, all featuring the fadolín, a unique string instrument that encompasses the range of the violin, viola and cello.

Justin Rothberg's trio performs a fusion of funky jazz, borrowing from various styles like hip hop, reggae, folk, and Afro Cuban.  With three albums and countless performances the trio plays a fresh take on classic pop songs as well as original music.

A “virtuoso extraordinaire on the violin” (Wynton Marsalis), Leonardo brings forth a new artistic vision with an “ingenious musical concept”  - a classical string quartet that “personifies and embodies the authentic spirit of Tango” (Latin Grammy). Instrumental & vocal program features Leonardo Suarez-Paz (violin I /voice), Francisco Salazar (violin II), Jon Weber (viola), Danny Miller (violoncello).

Yalin Zhuang is a classically trained pianist based in New York City. Though rooted in traditional classical training, her artistry reaches beyond the score. She explores the piano as a space for reflection, embodiment, and transformation. Her practice often weaves in mindfulness, improvisation, and movement — honoring both the discipline and the freedom of music-making. She believes music should not only be performed, but lived — and that the act of playing is also an act of listening, healing, and connecting. Co-presented with ALT Alliance.

Simón Gómez Villegas (they/all) is a captivating performer and composer hailing from Bogotá, Colombia. Blending musical theatre, indie pop, and Americana Shakespeare, Simón creates music that is as expansive as it is intimate. Simón has performed everywhere from Alaskan dinner theaters to early childhood enrichment programs. Past credits include LIFE FORCE (Mercury Store, The Team) THIRD SEX (ANT Fest 24’) and REAL WOMEN HAVE CURVES (American Repertory Theatre, Music Observer). Lyricist at the BMI Lehman Engel Musical Theater Writing Workshop. At Moynihan, they’ll be performing fan favorites from the last 70 years. Prepare to singalong if the spirit calls you!

Known for his expressive touch and commanding interpretations of virtuosic repertoire, pianist David Chang has performed at Steinway Hall, Lincoln Center, the National Opera Center, and venues across North America, Europe, and Asia. He discovered an extraordinary talent for teaching at just 13 years old and has spent over two decades helping adult students achieve results in months that typically take years. A graduate of Manhattan School of Music and NYU, David now coaches serious adult pianists and high performers around the world through his innovative virtual studio.
The Caracas Trio blends Afro-Venezuelan rhythms with jazz improvisation through a unique instrumentation. Their dynamic performances engage audiences in a journey through Venezuelan music and beyond.

Latin GRAMMY Award winner Pedro Giraudo and his Tango Quartet trace the evolution of Argentine tango, from its roots in the traditional orquesta típica, to the bold innovations of tango nuevo, epitomized by Astor Piazzolla, and now to a contemporary sound that honors the past while embracing the future. The quartet features Fung Chern Hwei on violin, Loïc da Silva on bandoneón, Emiliano Messiez on piano, and Pedro Giraudo on bass.

The Pablo Mayor Trio creates a dynamic tapestry of global Latin American rhythms, rooted in the innovative vision of Folklore Urbano NYC's founder and celebrated Columbian composer and pianist Pablo Mayor. With Carlos Mena on bass and Andrés Fonseca on percussion.
Don’t miss a live set by pianist, composer, and vocalist Martha Kato. Originally from Nagoya, Japan and now based in New York, Kato has performed internationally as a bandleader and collaborator.

Trio Fadolín performs an eclectic repertoire from around the world and across the ages, featuring music from Eastern Europe, Scandinavia, Latin-inspired grooves and much more, all featuring the fadolín, a unique string instrument that encompasses the range of the violin, viola and cello.  https://trio.fadolin.com
Harold O'Neal, hailed as one of this generation's greatest pianists and composers, delivers an unforgettable performance, blending the brilliance of Duke Ellington, Kenny Kirkland, and Maurice Ravel.

Boyd Meets Girl pairs Australian classical guitarist Rupert Boyd with American cellist Laura Metcalf. The duo has toured the world sharing their eclectic mix of music from Debussy and Bach to Radiohead and Beyoncé, and their two studio albums have received over 4 million streams on Spotify alone.

Friends since the age of 10, Rachel Handman (violin) and Keve Wilson (oboe) lead the Big Apple Band, made up of Broadway’s star musicians, entertains audiences with music from around the world. This oboe, violin, guitar, bass and drums combo spins irish jigs with Venezuelan joropos and Brazilian samba with Persian folk. Also featuring Matt Aronoff (bass), Justin Rothberg (guitar) and Mike Ramsey (percussion).

Neel Murgai (sitar) and Tripp Dudley (tabla) will be performing afternoon ragas and other delights from South Asian classical traditions and beyond. Neel and Tripp have a dynamic connection built from years of playing together in various incarnations with the Brooklyn Raga Massive, a musician's collective dedicated to raga and raga inspired music, where Neel is Artistic Director. Their latest group is the psychedelic raga rock band Quadrature, who will be releasing their debut album in 2025, showcasing their genre fluid expertise.

Drift Winds brings flutist Anjali Shinde, oboist Matthew Maroon, and bassoonist Marty Tung together with the simple goal of sharing meaningful music with as many people as possible. Their classical training pairs with a casual and open-minded nature, allowing Drift to connect across genre and locale to bring accessible slices of musical joy to your day. You'll hear a selection of their favorite musical selections, including their own arrangements, baroque and classical trio sonatas, opera arias, jazz standards, and more.
Adam Narimatsu is a NYC pianist with roots in New Orleans blues, stride, and jazz.

Award Winning Jazz Violinist Benjamin Sutin brings his vibrant blend of jazz, Latin, classic pop, and more to Moynihan Train Hall alongside Pianist Gabriel Schillinger Hyman. Together they reimagine timeless standards with spontaneity and soul. Expect an uplifting journey through swinging rhythms and heartfelt melodies.

Victoria Paterson leads Mostly Modern's hugely popular FAB5! She brings a rich diversity of musicians & styles with pop music on classical instruments, featuring the string quartet & cajon. Top songs and themes from BBC Planet Earth, The Crown, The Godfather, James Bond, Game of Thrones, Rocky, and more!

Friends since the age of 10, Rachel Handman (violin) and Keve Wilson (oboe) lead the Big Apple Band, made up of Broadway’s star musicians, entertains audiences with music from around the world. This oboe, violin, guitar, bass and drums combo spins irish jigs with Venezuelan joropos and Brazilian samba with Persian folk. Also featuring Matt Aronoff (bass), Justin Rothberg (guitar) and Mike Ramsey (percussion).

Arbor Duo, featuring soprano Marisa Karchin and classical guitarist Dr. Alberta Khoury, explores the essence of love and its countless expressions through a tapestry of languages and music across time. Their repertoire spans cultures worldwide, from Japan and Iran to Europe and Ladino traditions, weaving together diverse musical voices. United by a deep and lasting friendship formed at Juilliard, they bring passion and artistry to every performance, blending expressive storytelling with the guitar’s rich tonal colors and extended techniques.

Trio Fadolín performs an eclectic repertoire from around the world and across the ages, featuring music from Eastern Europe, Scandinavia, Latin-inspired grooves and much more, all featuring the fadolín, a unique string instrument that encompasses the range of the violin, viola and cello.  https://trio.fadolin.com
The Caracas Trio blends Afro-Venezuelan rhythms with jazz improvisation through a unique instrumentation. Their dynamic performances engage audiences in a journey through Venezuelan music and beyond.

Justin Rothberg's trio performs a fusion of funky jazz, borrowing from various styles like hip hop, reggae, folk, and Afro Cuban.  With three albums and countless performances the trio plays a fresh take on classic pop songs as well as original music.

Latin GRAMMY Award winner Pedro Giraudo and his Tango Quartet trace the evolution of Argentine tango, from its roots in the traditional orquesta típica, to the bold innovations of tango nuevo, epitomized by Astor Piazzolla, and now to a contemporary sound that honors the past while embracing the future. The quartet features Fung Chern Hwei on violin, Loïc da Silva on bandoneón, Emiliano Messiez on piano, and Pedro Giraudo on bass.

Friends since age 10, Rachel Handman (violin) and Keve Wilson (oboe) lead the Big Apple Band - a joyful ensemble of Broadway musicians who bring music from around the world to life. Their high-spirited mix spins Irish jigs with Venezuelan joropos, Brazilian samba with Persian folk, and more. For the holiday season, they add festive favorites and winter-bright arrangements to their globe-trotting sound, offering a warm, spirited celebration for all ages. Also featuring Matt Aronoff (bass), Justin Rothberg (guitar), and Mike Ramsey (percussion).

Justin Rothberg's quartet plays a high-spirited mix of pop, hip-hop, Afro-Cuban rhythms, and funky jazz that’s impossible not to move to. Featuring some of NYC’s most in-demand musicians on percussion, guitar, woodwinds, bass, and more. For the holiday season, they bring a festive groove and feel-good energy to Moynihan, serving up classics and surprises with New York flair and plenty of rhythm to keep spirits bright.

The renowned veteran ensemble Los Pleneros de la 21 brings to Moynihan Station its characteristic spirited New York style of Puerto Rican Bomba and Plena. Embracing the Holiday season the ensemble will treat their audience with infectious plenas, bombas, and aguinaldos. Bring comfortable shoes and get ready to join the parranda.

Latin GRAMMY Award winner Pedro Giraudo and his Tango Quartet bring the spirit of Argentina to Moynihan for tango made for winter nights — tracing the evolution of the form from traditional orquesta típica roots to the innovations of tango nuevo and today’s contemporary sound. Featuring Ali Bello (violin), Loïc da Silva (bandoneón), Emiliano Messiez (piano), and Pedro Giraudo (bass).

Brighten your commute with the joyful voices of LaGuardia High School’s celebrated Show Choir as they bring holiday classics and uplifting favorites to Moynihan Train Hall. Don’t miss these talented young performers spreading cheer and harmony throughout the busy season.

Pianist Amir Farid brings warmth and sparkle to the season with holiday favorites and timeless classics. A winner of the Australian National Piano Award and recipient of the Geoffrey Parsons Award for piano accompaniment, he shares a wide range of music - from beloved classical works to contemporary gems. His performances light up every space, from concert halls to welcoming community settings like Moynihan Train Hall.

Victoria Paterson leads Mostly Modern’s hugely popular FAB5! She brings a rich diversity of musicians and styles with pop music on classical instruments, featuring the string quartet and cajón. This December, FAB5! celebrates the season with a joyful holiday program that blends festive favorites and contemporary hits in their signature modern-classical style.

Trio Fadolín brings a global warmth to the season, performing music from Eastern Europe, Scandinavia, Latin traditions, and beyond. Centered around the fadolín - a unique instrument spanning the full range of violin, viola, and cello - their December performance blends holiday spirit, rich tradition, and captivating sound from around the world.

column4: Sing for Hope HandaHarmony Youth Chorus
column7: The Sing for Hope HandaHarmony Youth Chorus presents holiday music from around the world. This dynamic ensemble of young changemakers is trained through the Global Goals Arts Curriculum, a pioneering framework that integrates artistic expression with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. More than a chorus, HandaHarmony is a musical force for justice and unity—regularly performing at the United Nations and throughout the New York Metropolitan area. Recent highlights include performances for the Canadian Ambassador to the UN and President of the UN Economic and Social Council, Robert Rae, New York State Senator Jamaal Bailey, Mount Vernon Mayor Shawyn Patterson-Howard, and our Honorable New York State Attorney General, Leticia James.

Founded in 2014 by Benjamin Sutin, New York Music Daily once described Klazz-Ma-Tazz as "Epically Haunting Lynchian Klezmer Jazz." Klazz-Ma-Tazz explores a mix of new original music and uniquely reimagined modern jazz interpretations and arrangements of traditional secular Jewish, Eastern European and middle eastern music, catered to the nostalgically progressive audience of the 21st century. With Benjamin Sutin on violin, Elijah Shiffer on clarinet, Ben Rosenblum on accordion and Brian Glassman on upright bass

column4: Gaita! With Caracas Trio & Special Guests
column7: Gaita! Featuring members Caracas Trio joined by special guests, this joyful holiday program brings the spirit of Venezuelan gaita to Moynihan. Blending Afro-Venezuelan rhythms, jazz improvisation, and rich folk traditions, the ensemble creates a vibrant celebration rooted in the sounds of the season.

Friends since the age of 10, Rachel Handman (violin) and Keve Wilson (oboe) lead the Big Apple Band — a joyful ensemble of Broadway musicians who bring music from around the world to life. This lively oboe-violin-guitar-bass-drums combo blends Irish jigs with Venezuelan joropos, Brazilian samba with Persian folk, and more. For the holidays, they infuse their globe-spanning sound with festive warmth and winter cheer, inviting audiences to celebrate the season through rhythm, tradition, and musical joy. Featuring Matt Aronoff (bass), Justin Rothberg (guitar), and Mike Ramsey (percussion).

Simón Gómez Villegas (they/all) is a captivating performer and composer hailing from Bogotá, Colombia. Blending musical theatre, indie pop, and Americana Shakespeare, Simón "transforms the mundane into magic".  At Moynihan for the holidays, Simón will share festive favorites— sprinkled with storytelling, soul, and seasonal sparkle. Expect nostalgia, warmth, and plenty of moments to lift your voice and sing along.

Sing for Hope presents Sing Harlem, the breakout musical group from the Mama Foundation for the Arts. This highly respected and influential choir will light up the season with classic holiday songs that are sure to fill audiences with joy and festive spirit.

Adam Narimatsu is an NYC pianist whose December performances blend holiday favorites with New Orleans blues, classical interpretations, boogie-woogie, and stride piano. A longtime winter performer at the Empire State Building’s holiday celebrations, Adam brings his signature warmth and joyful piano traditions to Moynihan this season.
GREEN = SPECIAL HOLIDAY PERFS - ALTHOUGH ALL GROUPS WILL BE PLAYING HOLIDAY REPERTOIRE DURING DECEMBER
